business.headline: Good Friday holiday: Will NSE and BSE be open or closed on April 3?The stock market holiday list for 2026 includes a total of 16 non-trading days, excluding weekends. With several holidays already behind us, there are still multiple breaks lined up over the coming months.

Markets rebound: Sensex, Nifty recover losses after early crash. Here's whyEarlier in the day, both Sensex and Nifty had fallen nearly 2%, reacting to fresh comments from US President Donald Trump, who said the United States would hit Iran "extremely hard" in the coming weeks.

Form 15G, 15H gone: Will new Form 121 simplify TDS for taxpayers?Form 121 is a self-declaration that allows individuals to request that no TDS be deducted on certain types of income. This usually includes interest earned from bank deposits, post office schemes, or similar sources.

Will the new Income Tax website make filing returns simpler for you?The new website brings together all income tax-related information in one place. According to the department, it is easier to navigate, faster to use, and built to reduce confusion for taxpayers who often struggle with forms, rules and procedures.

8th Pay Commission moves ahead: Will Dehradun meeting bring your salary hike sooner?There's finally some activity around the 8th Pay Commission, and it's giving employees a reason to pay attention. With the Dehradun meeting scheduled, many are wondering, will this bring your salary revision any closer?

Gold, silver prices today: Why is silver falling more than gold?On the Multi Commodity Exchange (MCX), gold futures were trading at Rs 1,49,283 at 11:15 am, down Rs 4,425 or 2.88%. Silver futures saw a steeper fall. Prices were at Rs 2,28,891, down Rs 14,610 or 6.00%.

India's retail boom is hiding a Rs 2,000 crore problemIndia's retail boom is moving faster than ever on the surface, but inside, slow and messy inventory movement is quietly holding back efficiency, profitability and the next phase of growth.

Government waives customs duty on key petrochemical imports amid West Asia warThe Finance Ministry has reduced duty to nil on 40 petrochemical products, with the exemption coming into effect from April 2 and remaining in force till June 30, 2026.

Sensex crashes after Trump's speech on Iran war; Rs 10 lakh crore wiped outThe stock market saw a sharp sell-off, with the Sensex falling over 1,500 points and the Nifty dropping over2%, slipping below the 22,200 mark.

Markets sink as Sensex drops 1,300 points after Trump's speech on Iran warMarkets fell after US President Donald Trump's remarks on a possible escalation in the Iran conflict, which hit investor sentiment. He warned of strong military action, including potential strikes on oil infrastructure, even as he said diplomatic talks are still ongoing.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market crash after Trump's fresh speech?Early indicators suggest a soft start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 22,359 at 7:41 am, pointing to a gap-down opening for the Nifty 50, which had closed at 22,679.40 in the previous session.

PPF vs SIP: Where will Rs 50,000 grow more in 10 years?Not all investments grow the same way. A Rs 50,000 investment in PPF and SIP can lead to very different outcomes over a decade—raising a simple but key question: which one helps you build more wealth?

Iran war hits Indian bedrooms: How the conflict triggered a condom shortageAs the geopolitical tremors of the Iran war ripple through global supply chains, an intimate but essential public health commodity has become the latest casualty.

DA hike: What's causing the delay and how much can employees expect?The delay in the DA hike announcement has caught attention this year, especially among employees and pensioners who track it closely. But is this unusual, and more importantly, what kind of increase is likely this time?
Does your credit score matter when applying for a bank job?You may think your credit score only matters for loans, but could it also affect your chances of getting a bank job? Let's find out.

Sensex rallies over 1,100 points in a strong start to FY27The BSE Sensex rose 1,186.77 points, or 1.65%, to close at 73,134.32. The Nifty 50 gained 348 points, or 1.56%, to settle at 22,679.40, holding firmly above the 22,600 mark.

Blinkit brings quick commerce inside Mumbai Airport terminalsThe service allows travellers to order essentials directly through the Blinkit app and have them delivered anywhere within the departure area, including boarding gates, lounges, food courts, and select partner outlets.

entertainment.headline: ZEE5 to Sony LIV this week: Bhabiji film spin-off, Sitaare Zameen Par and more A fresh slate of OTT releases arrive this week across Sony LIV, Prime Video, Zee5, JioHotstar and Apple TV. From Sitaare Zameen Par, Bhabiji Ghar Par Hain: Fun on the Run to Maa Ka Sum, here's what to stream across genres.

Peddi: Ram Charan wields a mudgal in new poster released on Hanuman Jayanti A new poster of Ram Charan as a wrestler holding mudgal was released on Hanuman Jayanti. The film also features Janhvi Kapoor and Jagapathi Babu in pivotal roles.

Netflix this week: Mamla Legal Hai 2, Happy Patel, Vadh 2 and more Netflix dropped a fresh slate this Friday, blending comedy, action and courtroom drama. With new seasons and buzzy films, staying in this weekend looks like an easy win.

Actor Mustafa Ahmed says fans still think he is Rizwan of Dhurandhar 2 Actor Mustafa Ahmed spoke about fan reaction to his character, recalled key scenes and shared his dynamic with Ranveer Singh in an exclusive interview. He also opened up about how the blockbuster project came his way.

India's Rs 4000-crore, Ramayana goes global: Visionary leap or risky bet? Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana marks a bold chapter in Indian cinema with its ambitious scale and global strategy. The first glimpse of Ranbir Kapoor as Lord Rama in the film was released on April 2, 2026.

Dipika Chikhlia, OG Sita, reacts to Ramayana teaser: Very grand, awaiting the film The teaser of Ramayana has created a buzz. Actor Dipika Chikhlia, known for her role as Sita in the classic TV show, praised the teaser's richness and expressed her eager anticipation for the film's release.

Ramayana teaser review: Ranbir Kapoor as Rama cuts through the CGI noise Ranbir Kapoor steps into Rama in Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana, a teaser that blends scale with intrigue. It doesn't fully convince, but it reveals just enough to keep you watching, and questioning.

Producer Namit Malhotra reacts to India vs US launch debate after Ramayana reveal Ramayana director Nitesh Tiwari and producer Namit Malhotra opened up about the film's global rollout and cultural sensitivity. They also confirmed Ranbir Kapoor was always the first choice for Lord Rama.

Vijay alleges conspiracy in Jana Nayagan delay and Karur tragedy: I need justice Actor Vijay, while addressing a public meeting in Trichy, alleged conspiracy behind the Jana Nayagan hold-up and Karur stampede tragedy. He said that he's not only fighting for people's justice but also for himself.

Grossly vulgar: High Court orders removal of Honey Singh, Badshah's Volume 1 song The Delhi High Court has come down strongly on a controversial song linked to Yo Yo Honey Singh and Badshah, calling it "grossly vulgar" and ordering its immediate takedown. It also said that the song shocked the court to its core.

Neelira review: Someetharan's anti-war film is a portrait of war's human costRelease Date:Fri Apr 03, 2026 Neelira movie review: Director Someetharan's Neelira, starring Naveen Chandra, Roopa Koduvayur and Sananth Reddy, is a chamber drama set in the thick of the Sri Lankan civil war. The film is a straight-forward documentation of the effects of war on the lives of commoners.

Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla pushed amid Dhurandhar craze, set for paid previews Akshay Kumar's Bhooth Bangla has been postponed, with paid previews scheduled a day earlier. The decision comes amid Dhurandhar: The Revenge's strong box office run and screen dominance.

Amid Ramayana teaser buzz, Prabhas' Adipurush trends again. Here's why The recent teaser for Ramayana has not only generated excitement but also revived debates about Adipurush's cinematic treatment of the grand epic. Fans have flooded X with parody memes and comparisons.

Cannot wait: Karan Johar, Siddharth Anand and others react to Ramayana teaser Directors Karan Johar and Siddharth Anand, among other celebrities, have praised the teaser of Ramayana. The first part of the mythological film will release on Diwali 2026. It stars Ranbir Kapoor, Sai Pallavi, Yash, Sunny Deol and others.

Aakhri Sawaal teaser: Sanjay Dutt vs Namashi in a story of questions and conflict Aakhri Sawaal pits Sanjay Dutt against Namashi Chakraborty in a tale of questions and ideological conflict rooted in India's historical debates. The film is set to release on May 8, 2026.

Dhurandhar 2 actor Vikramm Bhambri says his scenes as Shakeel Commando were cut A Dhurandhar 2 actor reveals his scenes were cut from the final film, sharing his reaction and what changed his perspective amid the film's success.

Malayalam director Ranjith suspended by film body over sexual assault claims Malayalam director-producer Ranjith Balakrishnan was arrested by Kerala police following a sexual assault complaint. He has now been suspended by Film Employees Federation of Kerala (FEFKA).

Ramayana Part 2 shoot begins as VFX-heavy work on Part 1 moves full steam ahead The makers of Ramayana have quietly begun shooting Part 2 even as VFX-heavy post-production on Part 1 gathers pace. With indoor shoots and global-scale planning underway, the ambitious epic is moving faster than expected.

Ramayana teaser: Ranbir wins internet's heart, fans want more of Yash's Ravana Nitesh Tiwari's Ramayana teaser has wowed audiences with its grand visuals and Ranbir Kapoor's striking act as Lord Rama. While fans are loving the scale and craft, many are eager to see more of Yash's Ravana ahead of its Diwali release.

general news.headline: India attends UK's 35-nation summit on Hormuz reopening, Vikram Misri presentThe development comes as countries step up coordination to ensure stability in one of the world's most vital energy corridors. India is simultaneously engaging regional players, including Iran, to keep shipping lanes open.
Raghav Chadha shares video of his speeches after AAP demotes him in Rajya SabhaRaghav Chadha a video clip highlighting his remarks on multiple public issues in Parliament.

Job loss to AI, depression, secrets: Behind Bengaluru techie couple's suicideA job loss, a secret interfaith marriage, and fraying mental health pushed Bhanu Chander Reddy over the edge. His wife, unable to cope with the loss, followed him in death.

How Deloitte CA Raghav Chadha became Gen Z's favourite MPThe Aam Aadmi Party removed Rajya Sabha MP Raghav Chadha as the party's deputy leader in the Upper House. This is the story of Chadha, who became a CA at the age of 22, and became India's youngest Rajya Sabha MP.

Indian friends in safe hands: Iran on Strait of Hormuz blockadeIran has been highly selective in imposing its blockade on the Strait of Hormuz, with India being one of the very few nations whose cargo ships have been granted passage through the strategic waterway amid the current war in the Middle East.

A wet April ahead: Rain and hail to arrive next weekMarch was already riddled with sudden weather shifts and multiple western disturbance episodes, and now, even April is expected to experience the same. Find out if your region will experience rain and hail.

Macron hits back at Trump's 'wife beats him' remark: Neither elegant nor standardUS President Donald Trump has sparked a political storm in France after mocking French President Emmanuel Macron and his wife, drawing sharp reactions from Paris and rare cross-party criticism.

Video: Toddlers in Gaza hold doll's funeral, mirroring what they see every dayA funeral procession begins, not with grown men, but with toddlers. They carry a doll together on a stretcher, lifting it carefully as if it were real, moving in slow, measured steps. These children, who should be lost in ordinary games, instead recreate a funeral they seem to understand far too well.

How Raghav Chadha brought Reddit sabha to Rajya SabhaRaghav Chadha, the youngest Rajya Sabha MP, built a brand by raising issues that clicked with the urban middle-class. He debated issues in the Rajya Sabha that people generally discuss on Reddit. The AAP MP has a huge social media following.

Oracle layoffs not random? Ex-employee says algorithm targeted top talent with equityA viral LinkedIn post by a long-time Oracle employee has raised questions over whether the company's latest layoffs were driven by an internal algorithm. The claim suggests that high-performing staff with stock-heavy compensation may have been disproportionately impacted.

Four million Indians on this extramarital dating app. Bengaluru leads the wayA growing number of Indians are turning to discreet digital platforms to seek companionship, validation, or simply something missing in their existing relationships. And this is not just hearsay. Numbers are beginning to reflect that change.

Live: Middle East's highest bridge in Iran hit by air strike, 2 killedUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Iran-US-Israel conflict is intensifying, with tensions spreading across the region and new flashpoints emerging. The US military says it has now hit over 12,300 Iranian targets, including command and control centres, ballistic missile sites and storage bunkers, while more than 155 vessels have been damaged or destroyed. It also says over 13,000 combat flights involving advanced fighter jets and bombers have been carried out since the war began. On the ground, the situation remains volatile. An airport in Iran's Isfahan province has reportedly come under attack, while Kuwait says its air defences engaged three cruise missiles and 15 drones in the past 24 hours. Even as US President Donald Trump claims Iran has sought a ceasefire, Tehran has denied making any such proposal. Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said the Strait of Hormuz will not be opened to the enemies of this nation by the ridiculous displays of the US President. Trump is set to deliver his first primetime address on the war today, as attacks continue across the region. Meanwhile, Houthi rebels in Yemen fired ballistic missiles at southern Israel, describing the strike as part of a coordinated effort with Iran and Hezbollah. Stay with IndiaToday.in for the latest and live updates.

Iran charging toll to let Indian ships transit Hormuz? What government saidThe MEA's clarification came after Iranian state media reported three days ago that a parliamentary commission had approved a proposal to impose tolls on vessels transiting the Strait of Hormuz.

Feeling sleepy after lunch? Doctor explains why you shouldn't ignore itPost-meal fatigue can reflect how the body processes food, balances hormones and manages blood sugar levels. But if its too hard to control, happening every day, very frequently, you might want to get it checked by a doctor.

Women and the pocket gap: How fashion shrank storage and sold handbagsWomen once carried essentials in hidden waist-tied pockets, but changing fashion gradually made them disappear. Today's tiny or fake pockets are not random. Design trends, cost cutting, and gendered choices shaped this shift, leaving many women still relying on handbags instead of functional clothing.

5 reasons why Dhurandhar 2 dethroning Dangal sounds good for Indian cinemaAs Dhurandhar 2 eyes Dangal's long-standing box office record as the highest-grossing film in India, what would a new-era blockbuster topping the charts mean for Indian cinema's growth?

UP man bites wife's nose, cheek after fight; leaves her 10 stitches on faceThe man allegedly bit his wife's nose and cheeks during a domestic dispute, leaving her with grievous injuries that require advanced treatment and reconstructive surgery.

Live: Stalin accuses Centre of bias on metro projects, pitches Rs 8,000 scheme in CoimbatoreAssembly Election News 2026 Live Updates: Bengal Leader of Opposition Suvendu Adhikari is set to file his nomination papers from Bhabanipur today, mounting a direct challenge to Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee in her stronghold. Adhikari will be accompanied by Home Minister Amit Shah and other BJP leaders. In Tamil Nadu, TVK chief Vijay is set to file his nomination for the Trichy East constituency and will campaign in the city. Campaigning across five states and a UT gathered pace, with Prime Minister Narendra Modi leading rallies in Assam on Wednesday. Meanwhile, the Chief Electoral Officer's office in West Bengal issued a warning after Trinamool Congress workers staged protests over allegations that the BJP illegally submitted Form 6 applications to manipulate voter rolls. Follow indiatoday.in for the latest updates.

After Miami Open horror, Iga Swiatek hires Rafael Nadal's former coach Iga Swiatek has hired Rafael Nadal's former coach Francisco Roig after her shock Miami Open exit. Roig was a part of Nadal's team and briefly coached Emma Raducanu.

Australia set for first Zimbabwe visit in 8 years to prepare for ODI World Cup Australian cricket team will return to Zimbabwe for the first time in 8 years to play a 3-match ODI series. This series will act as key preparation for Australia's ODI World Cup defence.

After Ahmedabad, India to get second 1 lakh capacity cricket stadium Maharashtra's proposed 1 lakh-capacity stadium in the Mumbai Metropolitan Region aims to transform cricket infrastructure, boost tourism, and position the state as a global sporting hub. RCB pacer Nuwan Thushara drags Sri Lanka Cricket to court over IPL NOCRCB pacer Nuwan Thushara has taken Sri Lanka Cricket to court after being denied an NOC for IPL 2026, with the hearing scheduled for April 9, as he risks missing the season and potentially losing his spot in the squad.

Rassie van der Dussen retires after missing South Africa central contractRassie van der Dussen has called time on his international career after missing out on a central contract. The South African batter signs off as one of the most dependable middle-order players in white-ball cricket.

Messi's Argentina cheated Kerala: Sports minister lashes out at football teamKerala Sports Minister V Abdurahiman has accused Lionel Messi's Argentina of "cheating" the state after allegedly failing to honour a commitment to play a match despite receiving payment, triggering sharp reactions and raising the prospect of legal action.

Familiar conditions helped on the night, but a clearly defined role at Delhi Capitals could shape the consistency he has long sought.

Indian hopes fade early at Candidates 2026 despite promising start in CyprusIndia's Candidates 2026 campaign has shown promise but lacks consistency after four rounds. With Sindarov pulling ahead, the rest day offers a crucial reset for Praggnanandhaa, Divya and Vaishali.

SRH not feeling the absence of Pat Cummins on the field: Head coach VettoriIPL: SunRisers Hyderabad are not feeling the absence of their captain Pat Cummins on the field, said head coach Daniel Vettori. Vettori's claim comes at a time when SRH were blown away in their opener against RCB in their IPL opener.

What's gone wrong with Varun Chakravarthy? KKR coach explains spinner's slumpVarun Chakravarthy's dip in form has come under scrutiny, but KKR remain firm in their backing of the mystery spinner. Head coach Abhishek Nayar believes it is only a matter of time before he rediscovers his rhythm.

Brook, Bethell escape with warning after Wellington nightclub incidentHarry Brook and Jacob Bethell have been issued warnings after the Cricket Regulator concluded its probe into the Wellington nightclub incident. The episode, which surfaced months later, once again puts focus on England's off-field discipline.

The new unplayable: Lungi Ngidi's slower ball nears Bumrah yorker statusIPL 2026, LSG vs DC: On a night built for raw pace in Lucknow, Lungi Ngidi's slower ball stole the show by outfoxing Nicholas Pooran with a devilish late dip. Much like a Jasprit Bumrah yorker, it is becoming a piece of unplayable inevitability.

IPL 2026 Play Of The Day: Eureka! Badani's Stubbs call over Miller stuns LSGIPL 2026: The LSG vs DC game was decided outside the field as Hemang Badani had his eureka moment of sending Tristan Stubbs in ahead of David Miller. The move stunned LSG as DC romped to the win in the end with Stubbs guiding Rizvi and allowing him to play his natural game.

Is Mayank Yadav not fully fit to play in IPL? LSG batter reacts after DC gameMayank Yadav is fit but misses the LSG vs DC clash, reveals Lucknow batter Mukul Choudhary. Mayank last played in the IPL in May 2025.

Sangram Singh's second act: Taking Indian MMA to the world at the age of 40From wrestling champion to MMA pioneer, Sangram Singh aims to elevate Indian combat sports globally as he continues to defy the odds at the age of 40. Sangram hopes to act as a bridge for India to the MMA world and help up-and-coming talent.

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.
